titleStmt.title: Letter from MJ Murphy to Sir Edward O'Farrell, assistant under-secretary for Ireland, [November 1916?]
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: MJ Murphy

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter was written by M. J. Murphy to Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary to the lord lieutenant of Ireland.
The letter concerns the licence of a couple in Waterford City.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP 1916/695/18/6

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Monday Dear Sir Edward Very many
thanks for your letter.
Hyland is not initiated
in the spirit trade -
The licence has been
held since he married
(years ago) in his wifes
name. He is only
a Sleeper in the
House. He deals
in Cattle and works land. I have sent in two names
to Mr Birrell for the City of
Waterford and I'd be glad
all these could be fixed
together. The two I have
sent in are strong friends
of our Chief Mr. Redmond.
I requested Mr. Birrell to
ask Mr. Redmond's opinion
of them. My kind regards
Yours Faithfully MJ Murphy
